title: Directory expiry: prune pub_ids not seen in 90 days
description:
The relay directory is currently permanent — every pub_id that ever authenticated is kept forever. This means a long-lived relay accumulates a growing social graph even for users who have stopped using the service. Pruning inactive entries reduces the metadata footprint.

## Change
Add a periodic cleanup job in relay/src/store.rs:
  DELETE FROM directory WHERE last_seen < datetime('now', '-90 days')

Run at relay startup and every 24h via a tokio::spawn background task.

## Schema change
Add last_seen column to the directory table (migration v_next):
  ALTER TABLE directory ADD COLUMN last_seen TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT datetime('now')

Update last_seen on every successful authentication for the connecting pub_id.

## Configuration
Expiry window defaults to 90 days. Configurable via DIRECTORY_EXPIRY_DAYS env var.
Value of 0 disables expiry (opt-out for operators who want permanent directories).

## What this does NOT affect
- Active users: last_seen is updated on every connect, so active users are never pruned
- Offline message queue: pruning a directory entry does not delete queued messages (separate TTL)
- Subscriptions: subscription expiry is independent

## Defensive rules
- Deletion is permanent; log a count of pruned entries at INFO level: 'pruned N inactive directory entries'
- Run the cleanup in a spawned task with its own error handler; failure must not crash the relay
- The 90-day default is conservative; document the tradeoff (longer = more metadata retained, shorter = contacts appear to vanish)
close_reason: Added prune_inactive_users() to store.rs, DIRECTORY_EXPIRY_DAYS env var, startup+24h background task in main.rs, two unit tests
